items.title: The Claude C Compiler: What It Reveals About the Future of Software
items.why_it_matters: Simon Willison analyzes Claude as a C compiler, surfacing deep insights about where LLM-generated code is heading and what it means for software correctness.
items.action: Read Willison's analysis

items.title: We hid backdoors in ~40MB binaries and asked AI + Ghidra to find them
items.raw_summary: BinaryAudit benchmarks AI agents using Ghidra to find backdoors in compiled binaries of real open-source servers, proxies, and network infrastructure. (HN: 201 pts, 89 comments)
items.why_it_matters: BinaryAudit is the first serious benchmark for AI+Ghidra reverse engineering of compiled binaries — a major step for automated vulnerability discovery in closed-source software.
items.action: Try BinaryAudit on your own binaries

items.title: Samsung is adding Perplexity to Galaxy AI
items.raw_summary: In addition to summoning Bixby or Gemini, Galaxy S26 users will be able to call on Perplexity by saying "hey, Plex." The integration of Perplexity into Galaxy AI is just one element of the company's embrace of a "multi-agent ecosystem." Often, people will use different AI agents
items.why_it_matters: Samsung embedding Perplexity into Galaxy AI alongside Bixby and Gemini signals the shift toward multi-agent ecosystems on consumer devices.
items.action: Watch for Perplexity API integration patterns

items.title: Google restricting Google AI Pro/Ultra subscribers for using OpenClaw
items.raw_summary:
I’m seeking assistance regarding a sudden restriction on my Google AI Ultra account that has persisted for three days. I received no prior warnings or notifications regarding a potential violation. 
The only recent chang&hellip; (HN: 179 pts, 137 comments)
items.why_it_matters: Google restricting AI Ultra subscribers for using OpenClaw raises serious concerns about vendor lock-in and platform risk when building on proprietary AI APIs.
items.action: Audit your API dependencies for platform risk

items.title: How I use Claude Code: Separation of planning and execution
items.raw_summary: The research-plan-implement workflow I use to build software with Claude Code, and why I never let it write code until I've approved a written plan. (HN: 69 pts, 28 comments)
items.why_it_matters: A practical workflow for separating planning from execution in Claude Code — never let it write code until you've approved a written plan — resonates with production engineering discipline.
items.action: Adopt research-plan-implement workflow

items.title: New podcast on AI (full episode). Links below.
items.raw_summary:
New podcast on AI (full episode). Links below.

A Motorcycle for the Mind

0:00 If you want to learn, do

2:13 Vibe coding is the new product management

6:49 Training models is the new coding

10:13 Is traditional software engineering dead?

13:07 There is no demand for average

14:12 The hottest new programming language is English

18:36 AI is adapting to us faster than we are adapting to it

22:56 No entrepreneur is worried about AI taking their job

26:46 The goal is not to have a job

29:49
items.why_it_matters: Naval Ravikant's podcast frames vibe coding as 'the new product management' and English as 'the hottest programming language' — a signal of how mainstream AI-assisted development has become.

items.title: Excited to announce AI Research Skills - an open-source library of 82 specialized skills for AI codi
items.raw_summary:
Excited to announce AI Research Skills - an open-source library of 82 specialized skills for AI coding tools.

One command gives your agent expert knowledge in:
  → Model training & fine-tuning (TRL, Unsloth ...)
  → Distributed systems (DeepSpeed, FSDP ...)
  → Inference optimization (vLLM, TensorRT ...)
  → Agent building ? (Langchain, AutoGPT ...)
Works with Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI, Windsurf, and Codex with one click interactive installation

@ npx @orchestra-research/ai-research-skil
items.why_it_matters: 82 specialized AI research skills covering training, distributed systems, and inference optimization — drop-in expert knowledge for any AI coding agent.
items.action: Install AI Research Skills for your agent

items.title: AI uBlock Blacklist

items.why_it_matters: A community-maintained uBlock blacklist of AI-generated spam sites helps developers and users filter out low-quality AI content from search results.
items.action: Add the blacklist to your ad blocker

items.title: llmfit. Useful tool that probes hardware and tells you exactly which LLMs will actually run.
items.raw_summary:
llmfit. Useful tool that probes hardware and tells you exactly which LLMs will actually run.
- handles MoE expert offloading, picks the best quantization for your RAM, estimates tokens/sec before you even pull the weights.
Essential for local dev.
https://t.co/qn8PmqQNbV https://t.co/7z7yvOdNNC
items.why_it_matters: llmfit probes your hardware and tells you exactly which LLMs will run locally — handles MoE offloading, picks optimal quantization, and estimates tokens/sec before downloading.
items.action: Run llmfit before your next local model setup

items.title: 📦 openclaw/openclaw openclaw 2026.2.22-beta.1
items.raw_summary: ### Changes  - Provider/Mistral: add support for the Mistral provider, including memory embeddings and voice support. (#23845) Thanks @vincentkoc. - Update/Core: add an optional built-in auto-updater for package installs (`update.auto.*`), default-off, with stable rollout delay+j
items.why_it_matters: OpenClaw adds Mistral provider support with memory embeddings and voice, plus an optional auto-updater — expanding the open-source AI assistant ecosystem.
items.action: Try Mistral provider in OpenClaw

items.title: Software Engineer position will never die
items.raw_summary:
Imagine your boss pays you $570,000. Then tells the world your job disappears in 6 months.

That just happened at Anthropic.

Dario Amodei told Davos that Al can handle "most, maybe all" coding tasks in 6 to 12 months. His own engineers don't write code anymore. They edit what Al produces.

Meanwhile, Anthropic pays senior engineers a median of $570k. Some roles hit $759k. L5/L6 postings confirm $474k to $615k.

They're still hiring.

The $570k engineers aren't writing for loops. They decide whi
items.why_it_matters: The tension between Anthropic paying engineers $570K while its CEO says AI handles 'most coding tasks' captures the paradox at the heart of the current AI labor market.
items.action: Reassess your career positioning

items.title: 🚨 BREAKING: Singapore takes the lead again and publishes its Model AI Governance Framework for Agen
items.raw_summary:
🚨 BREAKING: Singapore takes the lead again and publishes its Model AI Governance Framework for Agentic AI [Bookmark it below]. Other countries should take note:

As the document clarifies, the new components of an agent create new sources of risk.

"The risks themselves are familiar – fundamentally, agents are software systems built on LLMs. They inherit traditional software vulnerabilities (such as SQL injection) and LLM-specific risks (such as hallucination, bias, data leakage, and adversaria
items.why_it_matters: Singapore publishes the first AI governance framework specifically for agentic AI, setting a regulatory precedent other countries will likely follow.
items.action: Review the framework for compliance planning
